text.title: rag-eval-ab-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for A/B testing RAG configurations.

rule.statement: Interleave A and B per question (A on q1, B on q1, A on q2, B on q2, ...). Never run all A then all B in two separate batches.
rule.rationale: LLM API performance and index state drift between batches; non-interleaved A/B confounds config differences with temporal effects.

rule.statement: Use separate indexes, separate caches, separate logging streams per config. Do not share state.
rule.rationale: Shared state leaks between A and B — second-runner sees first-runner's cache hits, producing artificially low latency on B.

rule.statement: Require at least 20 questions in the shared test set; warn if <50.
rule.rationale: Below 20, observed differences are within statistical noise; results are not actionable.

rule.statement: Report paired metrics per question (A_score, B_score, delta) plus aggregate paired-test p-value (Wilcoxon or paired t-test).
rule.rationale: Unpaired comparisons throw away question-level variance and inflate significance claims.

rule.statement: Promotion decision requires both: B's quality metric is significantly better AND latency is within +20% of A. A faster B that retrieves less relevant sources must not promote.
rule.rationale: Baseline RAGABTest only measures latency + source count; faster ≠ better.

rule.statement: Skip when the configurations are not isolated, the test set is <20 questions, or only latency matters.
rule.rationale: Non-isolated runs are unreliable; small N is noise; latency-only needs no A/B.
